summary: Matched and linked src/melee/it/items/itlinkarrow.c by switching itlinkarrow from NonMatching to Matching in configure.py, renaming fn_802A81C4 to itLinkArrow_802A81C4, adjusting symbol scopes/names for local data and sdata2 constants, correcting the itLinkArrow_802A850C prototype to return bool, and applying ordering/inlining-style source changes. The decomp.dev report showed +1144 matched-code bytes, +8904 linked-code bytes, +48 matched-data bytes, +336 linked-data bytes, and a new 100% match for itLinkArrow_802A850C, with a small noted vi0501 unmatched-item regression.
what_changed: Changed configure.py so melee/it/items/itlinkarrow.c is built as Object(Matching) instead of Object(NonMatching).
Renamed the function symbol at 0x802A81C4 from fn_802A81C4 to itLinkArrow_802A81C4 in both symbols.txt and itlinkarrow source/header declarations.
Reordered it_802A83E0 later in itlinkarrow.c and moved itLinkArrow_Logic98_Clanked after itLinkArrow_Logic98_HitShield, indicating source-order adjustments were part of matching/linking.
Added a static sdata2_ordering helper returning a volatile 0.0f in itlinkarrow.c, apparently to influence small-data/sdata2 ordering.
Changed itLinkArrow_802A850C from void to bool in itlinkarrow.c and itlinkarrow.h, and returned the bool result of it_8026EA20 from one branch.
Rewrote the itLinkArrow_802A81C4 body to use clearer locals z, temp, rand, and lookup_table, and indexed directly into it_803F6A84 rather than pointer arithmetic from it_803F6A28.
Changed itLinkarrow_UnkMotion4_Anim to return false rather than returning a local zero variable.
Updated GALE01 symbols for itlinkarrow-adjacent data: several previously global it_* or lbl_/jumptable names became local @NNN symbols, and a local ...data.0 label was added at 0x803F6A28.
In vi0501.c, introduced static getScale() returning 1.4285715f * un_804D6F80 and used it in un_8031D9F8, replacing the direct expression and removing PAD_STACK(4).
smart_moves: Used function return-type correction as a matching tactic: itLinkArrow_802A850C needed to be bool and return it_8026EA20's value, not void.
Preserved item-spawn logic while moving it_802A83E0 to the correct source/link order, avoiding behavioral churn while satisfying linker ordering.
Converted anonymous fn_802A81C4 into the subsystem-prefixed itLinkArrow_802A81C4 name, making callsites/comments easier to search and aligning with nearby itLinkArrow_* naming.
Matched data/link ordering by demoting generated symbol-map entries for local strings, jump tables, floats, and doubles to @NNN local labels instead of forcing global it_* names.
Isolated a scale expression in vi0501.c into getScale(), likely to alter codegen or sdata2 ordering without changing the arithmetic.
decomp_lessons: When an almost-matching function is off by a byte, check whether the decompiled signature should return a value; a void-vs-bool prototype mismatch can affect both codegen and header declarations.
Small helper functions can be used to control sdata2 ordering, but the evidence here only shows the added volatile-zero helper and symbol-map changes, not the full rationale.
Source order can matter for linking: moving complete functions rather than changing their logic may be enough to line up text/data layout.

Prefer direct table indexing from the real table symbol when possible; replacing broad pointer arithmetic from an earlier data address with &it_803F6A84[index] made the intent clearer while still matching.
Symbol-map cleanup is part of matching work: local constants, strings, jump tables, and labels should be represented as local @NNN/generated labels when they are not true globals.
A matching PR can include collateral improvements in nearby units to fix data ordering; here vi0501 .sdata2 reached 100%, though decomp.dev also reported a one-byte regression in un_8031D9F8.
naming_conventions: Use itLinkArrow_* for Link Arrow-specific functions once their ownership is known; this PR renamed fn_802A81C4 to itLinkArrow_802A81C4.
Keep address-bearing placeholder names such as it_802A83E0 when a semantic name is not yet established.
Use local @NNN symbol names in config/GALE01/symbols.txt for local strings, floats, doubles, and jump-table objects instead of promoting them to global it_* names.
The source has mixed capitalization already: itLinkArrow_* and itLinkarrow_* both appear in this slice; the PR only explicitly normalized fn_802A81C4 to itLinkArrow_802A81C4.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Changed Object(NonMatching, "melee/it/items/itlinkarrow.c") to Object(Matching, "melee/it/items/itlinkarrow.c") after source adjustments.
Adjusted function order in itlinkarrow.c to match the linked object layout.
Inserted sdata2_ordering with a volatile f32 zero to affect small-data constant placement/order.
Changed direct multiplication in vi0501.c into a static getScale() helper, which decomp.dev reported helped vi0501 .sdata2 reach 100%.
Returned the result of it_8026EA20 from itLinkArrow_802A850C and updated the prototype to bool, producing a reported 100% match for that function.
Updated symbols.txt scopes for data/sdata/sdata2 constants to local labels, including @265, @269, @297, @399, @639, @696, @821, @890, and @891 in .sdata2.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided slice.
decomp.dev reported matched code 71.51% (+0.03%, +1144 bytes), linked code 42.78% (+0.23%, +8904 bytes), matched data 41.44% (+0.00%, +48 bytes), and linked data 35.06% (+0.03%, +336 bytes).
decomp.dev reported 2 new matches: main/melee/vi/vi0501 .sdata2 reached 100.00%, and main/melee/it/items/itlinkarrow itLinkArrow_802A850C reached 100.00%.
decomp.dev also reported 1 regression in an unmatched item: main/melee/vi/vi0501 un_8031D9F8 changed from 98.83% to 98.76%, a -1 byte regression.
follow_up_queries: Find other PRs that convert item files from Object(NonMatching) to Object(Matching) in configure.py.
Search for other uses of volatile helper functions to force sdata2 ordering.
Look for void-to-bool prototype corrections that fixed one-byte item-function mismatches.
Compare Link item PRs for naming conventions around itLinkArrow, itlinkbomb, itlinkboomerang, and itlinkhookshot.
Investigate whether the vi0501 un_8031D9F8 one-byte regression was later recovered.
